docs.title: Welcome to the Unminal Engine
docs.text: 📘 Now you can see the official documentation for the Unminal Engine in front of you.

docs.title: Unminal Engine — Freedom to Will, Freedom to Act
docs.text:
• The engine doesn't dictate rules; it hands you the tools to bring your vision to life. From a flexible console system to rendering control — there are no restrictions here, only possibilities.
  • Help us keep this spirit alive: star the repo and watch Unminal evolve!
  • A simple, open-source 3D game engine built from scratch in C# using OpenTK.

docs.title: 🗺️ Features
docs.text:
1. Import objects with textures (UV system) *
  2. Update Text Renderer **
  3. Update Graphics 3.1 Shape Refactoring: reduction of creation in scripts *** 3.2 Light Refactoring: creating a separate object ****
  4. Implement AABB collision (at beginning only with built-in shapes) *****
  5. Create water object (visually, without collision) ***
  6. Particles **

Difficulty * Simple | ** Medium | *** Hard | **** So Hard | ***** Hardcore

docs.title: What's inside the documentation (soon)
docs.text:
• Architecture: How does the kernel, renderer, and console work?
  • Scripting: Writing logic in Lua (soon) and C#
  • Commands: Creating our own console commands with validation
  • UI & Rendering: Working with text, shaders, and the interface
  • ...and more!

docs.title: History of project
docs.text:
Originally, Unminal was conceived as a horror game set in The Backrooms universe. However, upon seeing countless similar projects, the author asked: "Why make yet another game when you can build a tool for thousands of others?"

And so Unminal Engine (formerly EOCS) was born — an indie engine crafted out of pure curiosity, evolving into a full-fledged platform for creativity.

docs.title: Getting Started with Unminal Engine
docs.text:
System Requirements

  • Operating System: Windows 10 or higher
  • Required: .NET SDK
  • Installation Guide: Microsoft Learn

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: There are two main ways to install the engine:

docs.title: Method 1: Via Git (Recommended)
docs.text:
This method allows you to easily update the project in the future.

  1. Install Git from the official website

  2. Open a terminal (PowerShell or Command Prompt)

  3. Clone the repository: 

    git clone https://github.com/dovintc-off/Unminal-Engine.git

  4. Navigate to the project folder: 

    cd Unminal-Engine

  5. Remember the path to the folder — you will need it to open the project in an IDE

💡 Advantage: Easy updates via git pull

docs.title: Method 2: Download ZIP Archive 📥
docs.text:
A simple way to get started quickly without using Git.

  1. Go to the repository page

  2. Click the green "<> Code" button

  3. Select "Download ZIP" from the dropdown menu

  4. After downloading, extract the archive to a convenient location

Tip

  • Windows 11: Built-in ZIP support
  • Windows 10 and older:
    • 7-Zip (Free, recommended)
    • WinRAR

docs.title: Recommended IDEs
docs.text: IDE Link Note Visual Studio Code Download Lightweight, requires extension setup Visual Studio 2022 Download Full-featured IDE from Microsoft JetBrains Rider Download Cross-platform IDE for .NET

docs.title: Setting up Visual Studio Code
docs.text:
If you choose VS Code, install the following extensions:

  1. C# Dev Kit — Core C# support
  2. .NET Install Tool — Manage .NET SDK
  3. IntelliCode for C# — Enhanced IntelliSense suggestions
  4. GitLens — Git integration (if using Method 1)

💡 Tip: For quick navigation, use Ctrl + Click to jump to method and class definitions.

docs.title: ▶️ First Run
docs.text:
1. Open the project folder in your chosen IDE
  2. Wait for NuGet dependencies to restore
  3. Locate the solution file .sln or project file .csproj
  4. Press F5 or click the "Run" button to start

docs.title: Having Issues?
docs.text:
• 📖 Read the full documentation
  • Report a bug on GitHub Issues

Info

After successful installation, check out the Engine Architecture section to understand the project structure.
